article_meta.title: Kid Rock Buries Bad Bunny in TMZ's Post-Super Bowl Halftime Show Poll 31 Chris Graythen/Getty ImagesWarner Todd Huston11

reasoning: The model correctly identifies several genuine bias instances in the author's own prose and avoids flagging quoted text. However, there are issues with bias type labels (using types not in the defined list) and some items are somewhat questionable. The model does a reasonable job identifying the strongly biased author prose in this Breitbart article, but uses invented bias type categories instead of the defined ones.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model correctly avoids flagging any quoted text. Item 5 references 'the poll probably wasn't going in the direction the gossip site thought it would' which could be considered a paraphrase of fan comments on X, but is presented as the author's framing/summary, so it's borderline acceptable. No clear attribution rule violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most flagged items are defensible as genuine bias in the author's prose. 'Kid Rock buried Bad Bunny' is genuinely loaded author language. 'Rabidly anti-Trump' is clearly biased word choice. TMZ manipulation claims are genuinely unsubstantiated. The vague statistics reference is a real issue. The social media framing item is somewhat weaker but still defensible as suggestive framing by the author.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model catches many key instances but could have also flagged: 'Kid Rock soared ahead' (loaded verb choice), 'Fans did not fall for the push' (implies TMZ was manipulating and fans resisted), the repeated use of 'gossip site' to describe TMZ (word choice/slant). Some notable instances were missed but the major ones were caught.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: This is the biggest problem. The model uses multiple bias type labels NOT in the defined list: 'Loaded Language' (should be Word Choice), 'Implied Motive' (should be Mind Reading), 'Accusation of Bias' (not a valid type - should be Unsubstantiated Claims or Spin), 'Speculation' (should be Mind Reading or Unsubstantiated Claims), 'Appeal to Authority (Unsubstantiated Statistics)' (should be Unsubstantiated Claims or Omission of Source Attribution), 'Suggestive Framing' (should be Spin or Slant). Nearly every label is from outside the defined list.
